titleStmt.title: Letter from Private C. Mumford to the Secretary, Dublin Castle, 22 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: C. Mumford

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Private C. Mumford of the 11th Battalion, Royal Inniskilling Fusiliers, to the secretary in Dublin Castle. Dependants of soldiers fighting during the Great War were entitled to a 'separation allowance' and Private Mumford, who had been wounded on active service and was recovering in London General Hospital, writes that his mother's allowance has been stopped. He enquires about rectifying the situation and a note on the letter suggests that he should contact the military authorities.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary’s Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/8789/1

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Monday 22. 5. 1916 14248 Pte. C. Mumford 11th Batt. Royal Inniskilling Fusiliers Ward C8 3rd London General Hospital Wandsworth Common London. S.W To the Secretary Dublin Castle Dear Sir I understand from my Mother that her
allowance has been stopped without explanation & I should feel
obliged if you could let me know the reason.
I might mention that my mother is a widow & the
cessation of the allowance is a source of considerate
hardship to her. Thanking you in anticipation
of your giving the case your attention. Yours truly Pte. C Mumford Ack and state that if the allowance
referred to is an Army Separation allowance
he should place himself in communication
with the military authorities.
